checks.detail: Slim base, cache cleaned, every installed package (build-essential, musl-tools/dev, git, tmux, strace, procps) is genuinely used by build or verifier.
Only solve.sh present; embeds the C source inline and writes the oracle marker correctly.
All major sections are justified by the threat model, but two lines are defensive duplicates of Dockerfile state.
reasons: useful: chmod 700 /tests (Harbor resets to 755 after upload)
useful: env -i + LD_PRELOAD/PYTHONPATH unset (single-container side channels)
useful: pkill -u agent + recheck (kills cross-phase background writers)
useful: root-owned $STAGE with .o/.a copies (agent cannot swap link inputs)
useful: strace -f around build + every link/run (mandatory: agent code executes)
useful: shellout detection via execve grep (catches exec families the source-substring scan can't see)
useful: trap to guarantee reward.txt exists on early exit
redundant: chmod -R go-rwx $REFDATA at line 49 duplicates Dockerfile line 86
redundant: find /tests -name '*.sh' -exec chmod +x — no .sh files live in /tests at runtime besides test.sh itself

checks.detail: No individual files exceed 30MB; repo is text-only sources plus small assembly/C test inputs.
Workspace contains only Makefile, myld.c, start.S, syscalls.S, and test inputs — no dead code or stale configs.

redundant_items.why: Dockerfile already applies chmod -R go-rwx /tests/refdata at build time; /tests is root-only so the agent cannot alter it between phases.
The only .sh in /tests is test.sh (already executable to be invoked); the runtime RUNNER script is created in $STAGE, not /tests.

redundant_items.item: line 152: duplicate 'reward' key alongside 'score' in reward.json payload
redundant_items.why: reward.json schema specifies only 'score'; the extra top-level 'reward' field is a stray key not in the documented schema.

notes: test.sh is dense but every section maps to a real attack vector given the single-container model — keep as-is or trim only the two defensive duplicates noted.
Consider moving the extra 'reward' top-level key in reward.json into additional_data to match the documented schema strictly.
